title: Semi-thin Section Analysis
author: Sue Lin
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zz5f786

steps.instruction: Double fixation: The specimen was first fixed with 2.5% glutaraldehyde in phosphate buffer (pH7.0) for more than 4 hours; washed three times in the phosphate buffer; then postfixed with 1% OsO 4 in phosphate buffer (pH7.0) for 1 hour and washed three times in the phosphate buffer.

Dehydration: The specimen was first dehydrated by a graded series of ethanol (50%, 70%, 80%, 90%, 95% and 100%) for about 15 to 20 minutes at each step, transferred to absolute acetone for 20 minutes.

Infiltration: The specimen was placed in 1:1 mixture of absolute acetone and the final Spurr resin mixture for 1 hour at room temperature, then transferred to 1:3 mixture of absolute acetone and the final resin mixture for 3 hours and to final Spurr resin mixture for overnight.

Embedding and semi-thin sectioning: Specimen was placed in capsules contained embedding medium and heated at 70℃ for about 9 hours. Semi-thin sections (1 μm) were sliced under a LKB 11800 PYRAMITOME ultramicrotome (Stockholm, Sweden) and stained with 0.5% toluidine blue. Images of the anther cross-sections were obtained with a Leica DMLB fluorescence microscope (Leica Microsystems, Wetzlar, Germany).
